headline: SILVER INSTITUTE DECLARES "SQUEEZE REGIME" AS SILVER NEARS $80/OZ — SIXTH STRAIGHT ANNUAL DEFICIT WIDENS TO 46.3 MOZ
snippet: The Silver Institute's World Silver Survey 2026, released April 15, projects the sixth consecutive structural deficit at 46.3 million ounces — wider than 2025's 40.3 Moz gap. Silver rose to $79.60/oz on April 17, approaching the $80 psychological level. Metals Focus warned that squeeze conditions are rapidly building as the share of unencumbered silver available in London vaults has fallen sharply.

headline: SENATE VOTES 50-49 TO END BIDEN-ERA BOUNDARY WATERS MINING BAN — TWIN METALS COPPER-NICKEL PROJECT CLEARS MAJOR HURDLE
snippet: The U.S. Senate voted 50-49 on April 16 to end the Biden administration's 20-year mineral withdrawal protecting 225,378 acres near the Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness in Minnesota. The resolution heads to President Trump, clearing the path for Antofagasta's Twin Metals — a proposed copper-nickel-cobalt mine near Ely — to reapply for federal mineral leases. Years of permitting review remain before any shovel hits ground.

headline: GOLD CLIMBS TO $4,867/OZ, UP 1.65% — PRECIOUS METALS RECAPTURE SAFE-HAVEN BID AS US-IRAN DIPLOMACY ADVANCES
snippet: Gold rose to $4,867.39/oz on April 17, up 1.65% from the prior session, as investors rotated back into safe-haven assets amid signs of renewed US-Iran diplomatic engagement that could ease broader market stress. Gold is up 41.73% year-over-year. J.P. Morgan forecasts average prices of $5,055/oz in Q4 2026, rising to $5,400/oz by end of 2027. Silver gained 1.52% to $79.60 on the same session.

headline: HARVEST GOLD (TSXV: HVG) MAPS 50 DRILL TARGETS ACROSS 32KM GOLD CORRIDOR IN QUEBEC'S URBAN BARRY BELT
snippet: Harvest Gold defined 50 drill targets along the 32km Kiask River Mineralized Corridor within its Mosseau/LaBelle property in Quebec's Urban Barry Belt, announced April 16. Twenty targets are prioritized for 2026, with 11 clustered along a critical 2km magnetic high feature. The campaign follows the company's February discovery hole of 105 g/t Au over 1.15m, one of the Urban Barry Belt's most exciting early-stage hits in years.

headline: SPROTT LAUNCHES WORLD'S FIRST EX-CHINA RARE EARTH ETF (REXC) ON NASDAQ — TRACKS NON-CHINESE PRODUCERS ONLY
snippet: Sprott Asset Management debuted the Sprott Rare Earths Ex-China ETF (REXC) on Nasdaq on April 14, the world's only ETF focused exclusively on rare earth companies operating outside China. The fund tracks the Nasdaq Sprott Rare Earths Ex-China Index and joins Sprott's critical materials suite covering uranium, copper, lithium and nickel. China controls roughly 85% of global rare earth refining capacity, making ex-China exposure a growing institutional priority.

headline: MOTLEY FOOL: USA RARE EARTH'S $1.6B GOVERNMENT LOI HIDES AN EQUITY DILUTION TRAP — WHY TO AVOID USAR
snippet: The U.S. government issued a $1.6B letter of intent to USA Rare Earth (USAR), but the terms allow Washington to retain its equity stake even if it never provides the funding — a structure that creates asymmetric dilution risk for common shareholders. USAR controls the Round Top deposit in Texas, home to heavy rare earths dysprosium and terbium. Motley Fool argues the deal's fine print fundamentally breaks the risk-reward for retail investors.

headline: LITHIUM CARBONATE NEARLY DOUBLES IN Q1 2026 TO $26,278/T AS SUPPLY DELAYS AND SPECULATIVE BUYING COLLIDE
snippet: Battery-grade lithium carbonate surged approximately 100% in Q1 2026 to $26,278/t as production delays at South American brine operations triggered speculative buying. Rio Tinto's Rincon project in Argentina secured $1.175B in multilateral financing but full production is years away. INN cautions that elevated volatility may persist through H1 2026 and the rally is susceptible to reversal on demand softness.

headline: URANIUM HOLDS $86/LB, UP 33% YEAR-ON-YEAR — NEXGEN'S ROOK I TO BREAK GROUND THIS SUMMER AS NUCLEAR SUPPLY CRUNCH DEEPENS
snippet: Uranium traded at $86.30/lb on April 16, up 32.67% year-over-year. NexGen Energy received its CNSC construction licence in March and is targeting summer 2026 groundbreaking at Rook I, Canada's largest development-stage uranium deposit, with a C$2.2B capex build. The U.S. has formally designated uranium a national security asset under Section 232, opening the door to price supports and import curbs on foreign supply.

headline: AMEX EXPLORATION PERRON GOLD FEASIBILITY: C$1.13B NPV, 114.6% IRR, 147KOZ/YR AT US$910/OZ AISC — AMONG THE BEST RETURNS IN SECTOR
snippet: Amex Exploration's Phase 1 feasibility study for the Perron Gold Mine in Quebec's Abitibi region outlines 147,000 oz/yr gold production over five years at a US$910/oz all-in sustaining cost. Post-tax IRR is 114.6% at an assumed US$3,500/oz gold price. Initial capex of C$193.9M with a 17-year mine life. At current gold prices well above US$4,800/oz, the actual economics are substantially more attractive than the base case.

headline: OSISKO METALS GASPÉ COPPER RESOURCE SURGES 119% TO 1.83 BILLION TONNES — 10.8 BILLION LBS CONTAINED COPPER IN QUEBEC
snippet: Osisko Metals' updated MRE for its Gaspé Copper project in eastern Quebec shows 1.83 billion tonnes M&I at 0.27% Cu — a 119% increase in contained copper to 10.8 billion lbs. The deposit also hosts 673 million lbs of molybdenum and 93 million oz of silver, ranking among North America's largest undeveloped Cu-Mo systems. A 50,000m follow-up drill program begins later this month targeting resource expansion.
